attacking_style: Operates from breakaways, targeting terrain between climbs rather than summit finishes. Shows willingness to attack solo on transitional sections, but lacks ability to sustain efforts on long final climbs.
team_tactics: Functions autonomously in breakaway scenarios with minimal visible team support. Engages in tactical negotiations with rivals (yielding stages for jersey considerations), suggesting calculated approach over raw aggression.
terrain_preference: Transitional terrain between climbs and breakaway-friendly stages suit best. Exposed on long, sustained final climbs where caught despite significant advantages (51sec lead evaporated in 8km).
vulnerability: Cannot defend advantages on extended final climbs against pure climbers. Caught 8km from finish on Etna despite substantial gap. Relies on breakaway math rather than climbing superiority.
key_patterns: Operates from multi-rider breakaways (14-man group) rather than solo efforts from peloton
Attacks on transitional sections between climbs, not on steep gradients or summit finishes
Engages in jersey negotiations and tactical bargaining within breakaways (yielded stage for classification)
Unable to sustain climbing efforts: 51-second advantage disappeared over final 8km of climb
form_trajectory: neutral: Won from breakaway but also showed climbing limitations when caught despite large gap. Mixed results suggest opportunistic rather than dominant form.
